segments.text: Job's three friends stopped responding to him because he kept on protesting his innocence.
Then Elihu, the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram, grew angry. He was angry with Job for claiming he was right rather than God.
Elihu was also angry at Job's three friends because they made it seem that God was wrong, because they had not been able to answer Job. [^212]
Elihu had waited for the other three to speak with Job since they were older than he was.
But when he saw that they could not answer Job, he grew very angry.
Elihu, the son of Barachel the Buzite, said, {
I am young, and you are old, which is why I was reluctant to tell you what I know.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I told myself, ‘Those who are older should speak—those who are elderly should teach wisdom.’
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: However, there is a spirit[^213] in human beings, the breath of the Almighty, that gives them understanding.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: It is not the old who are wise, or the elderly who know what is right.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: That's why I'm telling you to listen to me now—let me tell you what I know.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Well, I waited to hear what you had to say, listening for your insights as you looked for the right words to speak.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I paid close attention to all of you, and none of you have proved Job wrong or answered his arguments.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Don't say to yourselves, ‘We are so wise,’[^214] for God will prove him wrong, not a human being.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Job didn't line up his arguments against me, and I won't answer him the way you did.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: You sit there speechless[^215] with nothing left to say.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Should I continue to wait since you're no longer talking, just standing there saying nothing?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: No—now I'll give my answer too. I'll tell you what I know.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I have so much to say I can't hold the words back! [^216]
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Inside I'm like fermenting wine all bottled up; like new wineskins ready to explode!
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I have to speak before I burst; [^217] I will open my lips to answer him.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I will not take sides, and I'm not going to flatter anyone.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I don't know how to flatter, and if I did my Creator would soon destroy me.
segments.speaker: Elihu
